:::{#lem-odds-from-logodds}
#### Odds from log-odds

::: notes
If $\odds$ is the odds of an event $A$
and $\logodds$ is the corresponding log-odds of $A$,
then:
:::

$$\odds = \expf{\logodds}$$

:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

::: proof
Starting from @def-logodds, we solve for $\odds$:

$$
\ba
\logodds &\eqdef \logf{\odds}
&& \text{(definition of log-odds)}
\\ \expf{\logodds} &= \expf{\logf{\odds}}
&& \text{(exponentiate both sides)}
\\ \expf{\logodds} &= \odds
&& \text{(the exponential function inverts the natural logarithm)}
\\ \odds &= \expf{\logodds}
&& \text{(swap the two sides of the equation)}
\ea
$$
:::
